Public Service Announcement
ᑭᒃᑯᑐᐃᓐᓇᕐᓄᑦ ᑐᓴᕋᑦᓴᐃᑦ ᐊᑐᐃᓐᓇᐃᑦ ᐃᓄᒃᑎᑐᑦ, ᖃᓪᓗᓈᑎᑐᑦ ᐃᓄᐃᓐᓇᖅᑐᓐ ᐅᐃᕖᑎᑐᓪᓗ ᐅᕙᓂ www.gov.nu.ca.
Public Service Announcements are available in Inuktitut, English, Inuinnaqtun and French on www.gov.nu.ca.
Kavamatkunnin Tuhaqtitaujukhat hailihimajun Inuktitut, Qablunaatun, Inuinnaqtun Uiuitullu uvani www.gov.nu.ca.
Les messages d’intérêt public sont disponibles en inuktitut, en anglais, en inuinnaqtun et en français au www.gov.nu.ca.
Communications
RSV prevention program in Nunavut
Start Date: December 13, 2024
End Date: January 13, 2025
Nunavut-wide 60 sec
The Department of Health would like to advise Nunavummiut of changes to the
Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) prevention program.
Previously, Nunavut offered a monoclonal antibody called SYNAGIS to the highest risk
infants. This product needed to be given once a month during the RSV season.
After extensive review, Health Canada has approved new products for both infants and
Elders. Based on the recommendation of the National Advisory Committee on
Immunizations of these newer, more effective products, Nunavut is now able to offer a
longer-acting monoclonal antibody called BEYFORTUS to all Nunavut infants at or
shortly after birth.
Additionally, Nunavummiut 75 years of age and older are being offered a one-time dose
of a vaccine called ABRYSVO that can prevent RSV.
For more information about vaccines talk to your health care provider.
###
